info.name: RAG Demo - Tour Recommendation Agent
info.description: Demo endpoints for the RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) flow with Spring AI + Gemini + pgvector. Run order: index the catalog first, then trigger an order. All ids follow the UUID pattern.

request.description: INDEXING. Simulates 'product created/updated' events from the Products service and indexes the whole sample tour catalog (21 tours across Rome, Paris and London) into the vector store.

body.raw:
{
  "productId": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000022",
  "name": "Sainte-Chapelle & Conciergerie Guided Visit",
  "description": "Admire the stunning medieval stained-glass windows of Sainte-Chapelle and the historic Conciergerie.",
  "location": "Paris, France",
  "category": "History & Culture",
  "price": 44.0,
  "currency": "EUR",
  "tags": ["history", "art", "guided"]
}

request.description: INDEXING. Simulates one 'product created' event from the Products service.
RETRIEVAL + GENERATION. Simulates the fixed 'order created' event (Colosseum tour in Rome) and returns the RAG recommendation.

RETRIEVAL + GENERATION. Custom order. orderName drives the inferred interest and orderLocation constrains recommendations to the same city/region. Reuse the same customerId across orders to demonstrate the purchase-history effect.

item.name: 5. Retrieval + Generation - second order, same customer

request.description: RETRIEVAL + GENERATION. After steps 3/4, this order's retrieval already sees the customer's history, so the agent avoids recommending previously purchased tours while staying within the same region (Rome).
RETRIEVAL + GENERATION. A different customer buying a history tour in London. Demonstrates that the location constraint keeps the recommendations within the same city.
